login.title: Sign in
login.googleFailed: Google sign-in failed. Please try again.
login.microsoftFailed: Microsoft sign-in failed. Please try again.
login.marketingDesc: OnePass is the all-in-one badge scanning & activity tracking platform for in-person events. Create activities, invite your team, scan participants at the door — and get real-time analytics, CSV exports, and a fully offline-capable progressive web app.
login.marketingFeature2: Works offline — scans sync when back online

register.title: Create an account
register.usernameTooShort: Username must be at least 3 characters.

activity.name: Name
activity.duplicateName: An activity with this name already exists.
activity.resetScansConfirm: Delete all participants and scans for activity "{{name}}"?
activity.cannotDeleteLast: At least one activity must always exist.

scan.title: Scan participant
scan.offlineQueued: You are offline. Scan will sync when reconnected.
scan.cameraHint: Point your camera at a QR code
scan.duplicate: This participant has already been scanned for this activity.
scan.duplicateAt: This participant has already been scanned for this activity on {{when}}.

parameters.title: Settings
parameters.eventNameHelp: Public-facing name shown across the application.
parameters.defaultActivityHelp: Pre-selected on the Scan page when a user has not chosen their own default.
parameters.myDefaultActivityHelp: Overrides the global default for your account.

participants.deleteConfirm: Delete participant "{{name}}" and all of their scans for this activity?

globalAdmin.title: Global administration
globalAdmin.subtitle: Settings reserved for the OnePass platform operator. Visible to global Admin accounts only.
globalAdmin.maintenancePlaceholder: e.g. Scheduled maintenance on Sunday from 02:00 to 03:00 UTC…
globalAdmin.defaultLimits: Default fair-use limits for new organisations
globalAdmin.orgsNone: No organisations have been created yet.

step1.title: Step 1 · Your organisation
step1.lead: Let's set up your tenant. The organisation owns events, members and branding.

step2.title: Step 2 · Your first event
step2.lead: An event groups the activities running together — a conference, expo or internal day.

step3.title: Step 3 · Default activity
step3.lead: Activities are the scannable things inside an event. You can skip this and add activities later.

step4.title: Step 4 · Review
step4.lead: Almost done. Review the choices below and we'll create everything in one go.

org.name: Organisation name
org.slugHint: Lower-case, dashes only. Leave blank to derive it from the name.

activity.name: Activity name
activity.optional: Optional — leave blank to add activities later.

help.title: Help & glossary
help.intro: OnePass organises in-person events as a small hierarchy: an Organisation hosts Events, each Event groups Activities, every Activity has Participants, and a Scan is recorded each time a Scanner badges a Participant for an Activity.

org.title: Organisation
org.desc: The top-level tenant. Owns events, members, branding and fair-use limits. You can be a member of several organisations and switch between them from the header.
org.bullet2: Owned by: the user who created it (Owner role).

event.title: Event
event.desc: A time-bounded gathering inside an organisation — a conference, expo, internal hackathon…
event.bullet2: Contains: one or more Activities, plus a default activity used by the Scan page.

activity.title: Activity
activity.desc: A scannable thing inside an event: a booth game, a workshop, an entry checkpoint, a meal counter… Activities define when scans are accepted and how many scans each participant may receive.
activity.bullet1: Example: CraneGrabberClawMachine — the booth claw-machine game where each attendee is allowed exactly one play.
activity.bullet2: Time window: Starts / Ends timestamps gate when scans are accepted.
activity.bullet3: Max scans per participant: 1 by default, or unlimited.

participant.title: Participant
participant.desc: A person who can be scanned for an activity. Identified by a badge code (QR / barcode) and an optional display name and email.
participant.bullet1: Example: Alice with badge A-001 attending Devoxx.
participant.bullet2: Created: ahead of time by import, or on the fly the first time their badge is scanned.

scan.title: Scan
scan.desc: The atomic event recorded each time a Scanner badges a Participant for an Activity. Always carries who scanned, when, and from which device.
scan.bullet1: Scans are rejected if the activity window is closed or the per-participant cap is reached.
scan.bullet2: Offline scans are queued in the browser and replayed when connectivity returns.

user.title: User & roles
user.desc: A signed-in account. Each membership in an organisation carries one role:

role.admin: Admin / Owner — full configuration, can invite members and manage events, activities and users.
role.scanner: Scanner — can record scans on the Scan page; cannot reconfigure the event.

invitation.title: Invitation
invitation.desc: A one-time link an Admin sends to add someone to an organisation with a chosen role. Invitations expire and can be revoked.

help.exampleIntro: Putting it all together with a fictitious tenant:
help.exampleNarrative: Microsoft (Organisation) sponsors a booth at Devoxx (Event). At the booth they run the CraneGrabberClawMachine activity, capped at one scan per badge so each attendee gets exactly one chance. A scanner-role staffer stands at the booth, opens the Scan page, and badges every player. Each scan creates a new Participant on first sight, then records the scan. Admins watch totals on the Dashboard in real time and export the CSV at the end of the day.

workflow.step1: Create an Organisation (or accept an invitation to one).
workflow.step2: Create an Event for the conference / gathering.
workflow.step3: Add one or more Activities with their time window and per-participant scan cap.
workflow.step4: Invite staff with the right role (Scanner for booth helpers, Admin for organisers).
workflow.step5: On the day, scanners open the Scan page and badge participants — new participants are created automatically.
workflow.step6: Admins follow live counters on the Dashboard and export results from there.
